output: Batch 2 executed. Same-kind track reorder logic is now covered by targeted vitest tests, and TimelineEditor now has nested track-level dnd-kit scaffolding with separate visual/audio SortableContext groups plus Pointer and Keyboard sensors. Targeted vitest and eslint passed, and the batch checkpoint was written to `.megaplan/plans/fix-track-reordering-bugs-and-20260326-1856/execution_batch_2.json`.
deviations: Pulled forward the minimal `handleMoveTrack` hook/context plumbing required for T4's nested track DnD to compile and route drag-end events. The old `handleReorderTrack` path remains in place; TrackLabel was not converted to sortable in this batch.

task_updates.executor_notes: Extracted the arrow-button reorder logic into the pure helper `reorderTracksByDirection` and added a focused vitest suite that covers mixed kinds, single-of-each-kind, and single-kind-only configurations. Verification: `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineTrackManagement.test.ts` passed with 3/3 tests.

TimelineEditor now wraps the track label list in a nested dnd-kit `DndContext`, uses distinct `PointerSensor` and `KeyboardSensor` instances with `sortableKeyboardCoordinates`, and renders separate visual/audio `SortableContext` groups. Drag-end handling validates same-kind moves before calling `handleMoveTrack`. Verification: targeted eslint passed on the touched TimelineEditor and hook/state files.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Yes. The new test file covers all three required configurations: mixed kinds `[V1,V2,A1,A2]`, single-of-each-kind `[V1,A1]`, and single-kind-only `[V1,V2,V3]`. The reorder logic is now directly testable as the pure helper `reorderTracksByDirection`.

Yes. TimelineEditor now contains two separate `SortableContext` groups, one for visual tracks and one for audio tracks, inside a nested `DndContext` configured with both `PointerSensor` and `KeyboardSensor` using `sortableKeyboardCoordinates`.
